page: Amazon Web Services m8idb.2xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by an Intel processor with an x86_64 architecture. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs derived from 4 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, running at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The instance is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB per physical core.

This instance includes 474 GB of local NVMe SSD instance store volume, offering high-speed local storage directly attached to the host. It operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, which minimizes virtualization overhead. The network configuration provides a baseline bandwidth of 8 Gbps, and the instance does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

The m8idb.2xlarge offers a balanced resource profile suitable for workloads requiring a mix of compute, memory, and high-speed local storage. The inclusion of local NVMe SSDs provides qualitative cost efficiency for applications that benefit from low-latency temporary storage without the added cost of external block storage. This hardware profile is appropriate for general-purpose applications, web serving, containerized microservices, and small to medium database workloads that require fast local scratch space.
description: Amazon Web Services m8idb.2xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring an Intel x86_64 processor with 8 dedicated vCPUs and 4 physical cores running at 3.9 GHz. It is configured with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB per core ratio. A key hardware feature is the inclusion of 474 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage, which provides high-speed, low-latency local data access. Built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, the instance delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 8 Gbps. This resource configuration is designed for workloads that require a balance of compute and memory alongside fast local storage, such as web serving, containerized applications, and database hosting.
